shortAnswer: Cortisol is the adrenal gland's most important stress hormone and follows a daily rhythm with the highest values in the morning. Persistently elevated values can indicate chronic stress or adrenal overactivity, low values can indicate underactivity.
metaDescription: Cortisol too high or too low? Meaning, normal ranges, and causes explained simply.
doctorQuestions: Does my cortisol value match the time of day the blood was drawn?
